free_text: Excitation functions of proton induced nuclear
reactions on natural tungsten up to 34 MeV
Main reference.
Preliminary results for reaction
natW(p,x)186gRe.
MGC-20E cyclotron
CGR-560 cyclotron
Natural W foils, thicknesses: 10.74 and 25 micron.
Primary energies of the proton beams: 18 and 34 MeV,
measurements started: 20 and 50 h after the end of
bombardment.
The stacks were irradiated in a target holder which
served also as a Faraday-cup to determine the
integrated charge of the incoming beam.
The incident beam energy was determined by magnetic
analysis in Debrecen and by the parameters of the
acceleration and of the extraction calibrated by
magnetic deflection and time of flight method in
Brussels.
The irradiated foils were measured without any
chemical processing.
The energy degradation in the stack as a function of
the depth was determined by calculation based on the
well defined input energy and target thicknesses.
Decay data were taken from NuDat database.
A Cu foil was inserted in front of each stack (where
the beam energy is most exactly defined) to control the
beam intensity.
Chapter 4
Determination of the energy error was not
specified but earlier reports of the authors were
referred.
Determination of the total error was not
specified but earlier reports of the authors were
referred.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
The measured experimental data were compared to
and analyzed with theoretical calculations based on
the code ALICE-IPPE, taken from MENDL-2P database.
On the basis of the new experimental cross
sections integral yields were calculated.
